keys.description: Active provider config list for a tenant. Read by MarketingEventDispatcher on every fan-out to compute eligible providers. Hot path — every captured event hits this cache.
Eligible providers for (tenant, event_type). Sub-index of the tenant provider list, filtered to configs whose enabled_event_types includes the event_type. Read once per captured event.
invalidatedBy: MarketingProviderConfigObserver.* (invalidates every event_type sub-key for the tenant)

keys.description: Circuit-breaker state per (tenant, provider). Read by CircuitBreakerGate on every pre-dispatch. Sub-second TTL because the state changes frequently.
Rate-limit counter per (tenant, provider) per second. Fixed-window counter. TTL = 2s so a request that arrives at the boundary reads the current window's counter.
Memoises PiiHasher::hash($input) results. Small TTL because we don't want to hold hashed PII in cache indefinitely (compliance).

DomainEventMapper registry — the discovered mapping of domain-event-class → marketing-event-type. Read at boot into an in-memory registry via MarketingEventCapturer; this cache is a warm-boot accelerator.
Unresolved dead-letter count per tenant. Feeds the SDUI dead-letter-count badge.

Materialised ROAS report per (tenant, date range, grouping). Fed by the GET /roas endpoint + the monthly-report scheduled job. Long TTL because the underlying event ledger is append-only for delivered events; recomputation is expensive.
invalidatedBy: manual: marketing:invalidate-roas (rare — typically only after a bulk correction)

keys.description: Trailing 7-day baseline for consent-suppression rate detection. Read by the marketing:detect-consent-suppression-spike scheduled command.
Parsed data/providers/<provider>-config.schema.json JSON Schema object. Read by MarketingProviderConfigObserver on every config create/update. Loaded once at boot + rarely invalidated.
